content.body: A set is a well-defined collection of distinct objects. The objects in a set are called elements or members. Sets are usually named with capital letters like A, B, or C.

content.title: Types of Sets
Subsets
content.body:
Set B is a subset of A (B ⊆ A) if every element of B is also in A.
Example: If A = {1,2,3,4,5} and B = {2,4}, then B ⊆ A
Every set is a subset of itself: A ⊆ A
The empty set is a subset of every set: ∅ ⊆ A
The cardinality (number of elements) of a set A is written n(A). If A = {a, b, c, d}, then n(A) = 4. The empty set has cardinality zero: n(∅) = 0.

questions.question: Which of the following is an empty set?
questions.explanation: { } is the empty set notation. {0} contains the element 0, and there is one even number between 1 and 3 which is 2.

questions.question: If A = {2, 4, 6, 8}, what is n(A)?
questions.explanation: n(A) is the cardinality — the number of elements. A has 4 elements: 2, 4, 6, 8.

questions.question: Which symbol means 'is an element of'?
questions.explanation: ∈ means 'is an element of'. Example: 3 ∈ {1,2,3}.

questions.question: If B = {1, 3, 5} and A = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}, is B ⊆ A?
options: No, because B has odd numbers only
Yes, every element of B is in A
No, A and B must be equal
questions.answer: Yes, every element of B is in A
questions.explanation: B ⊆ A because 1∈A, 3∈A, 5∈A — every element of B belongs to A.

questions.question: What is the set of vowels in the word EDUCATION?
questions.explanation: The vowels in EDUCATION are E, D, U, C, A, T, I, O, N → vowels only: E, U, A, I, O → as a set {E, U, A, I, O}. Note: sets have no duplicates.

questions.question: The empty set is a subset of:
questions.explanation: By definition, the empty set ∅ is a subset of every set.

questions.question: Which set is infinite?
questions.explanation: Whole numbers (0,1,2,3,...) go on forever — that is an infinite set.

questions.question: If A = {a, b, c}, which of the following is TRUE?
questions.explanation: A has 3 elements (a, b, c), so n(A) = 3.

questions.question: Which notation describes the set of even numbers?
questions.explanation: Even numbers are divisible by 2: 2, 4, 6, 8, ...

questions.question: Is {3, 5} ⊆ {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}?
options: No, it must contain consecutive numbers
questions.explanation: 3 and 5 are both in the second set, so {3,5} is a subset.

content.body: Set operations allow us to combine or compare sets. The three most important operations are Union (∪), Intersection (∩), and Complement (A').

Union (A ∪ B): all elements that are in A OR in B (or both)
Intersection (A ∩ B): elements that are in BOTH A AND B
Complement (A'): all elements in the Universal Set that are NOT in A

content.title: Union and Intersection
Complement

content.body:
Useful rules:
n(A ∪ B) = n(A) + n(B) − n(A ∩ B)
This avoids counting shared elements twice!

questions.question: If A = {1,2,3} and B = {3,4,5}, what is A ∪ B?
questions.explanation: Union combines all elements with no repeats: {1,2,3,4,5}

questions.question: If A = {1,2,3} and B = {3,4,5}, what is A ∩ B?
questions.explanation: Intersection contains only elements in BOTH sets. Only 3 appears in both A and B.

questions.question: ξ = {1–10}, A = {1,3,5,7,9}. What is A'?
questions.explanation: A' contains elements in ξ (1-10) that are NOT in A (odd numbers), so A' = even numbers {2,4,6,8,10}.

questions.question: n(A)=12, n(B)=9, n(A∩B)=4. Find n(A∪B).
Two sets have no elements in common. What is their intersection?
questions.explanation: If no elements are shared, the intersection is empty: ∅. These are called disjoint sets.

questions.question: A = {vowels}, B = {a,b,c,d,e}. Find A ∩ B.
questions.explanation: Vowels = {a,e,i,o,u}. Elements in both sets: a and e are vowels AND in B.

questions.question: What does A ∪ A' equal?
questions.explanation: A and its complement A' together cover ALL elements in the universal set.

questions.question: What does A ∩ A' equal?
questions.explanation: A set and its complement share no elements, so their intersection is empty.

questions.question: If A ⊆ B, what is A ∩ B?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, then every element of A is already in B, so their intersection is just A.

content.body: Venn diagrams are visual representations of sets. A rectangle represents the Universal Set (ξ). Circles inside the rectangle represent individual sets. Overlapping circles show elements shared between sets.

content.title: Reading a Venn Diagram

content.body:
From a Venn diagram you can read:
• n(A only) = n(A) − n(A∩B)
• n(B only) = n(B) − n(A∩B)
• n(A∪B) = n(A only) + n(A∩B) + n(B only)
• Elements outside = n(ξ) − n(A∪B)

content.title: Problem Solving with Venn Diagrams
content.body:
In a class of 40 students: 25 study French, 20 study Kiswahili, 10 study both.
Find the number who study French only.
French only = 25 − 10 = 15
Kiswahili only = 20 − 10 = 10
Neither = 40 − (15 + 10 + 10) = 5

Venn diagrams with three overlapping circles follow the same principles but track seven regions: A only, B only, C only, A∩B only, A∩C only, B∩C only, and A∩B∩C (center). This method is used to solve complex real-world classification problems.

questions.question: In a Venn diagram, what does the rectangle represent?
questions.explanation: The rectangle in a Venn diagram always represents the Universal Set ξ.

questions.question: The overlapping region of two circles represents:
questions.explanation: The overlapping (shaded middle) region shows elements belonging to both A and B — the intersection.

questions.question: 30 students play football, 20 play basketball, 8 play both. How many play football only?
questions.explanation: Football only = 30 − 8 = 22

questions.question: Using the same data: n(football ∪ basketball) = ?
In a class of 50: 28 like maths, 22 like English, 10 like both. How many like neither?
Elements OUTSIDE both circles but inside the rectangle belong to:
questions.explanation: Elements inside ξ but outside both circles belong to the complement of A∪B, written (A∪B)'.

questions.question: n(ξ)=50, n(A∪B)=38. How many elements are outside both A and B?
questions.explanation: Elements outside = n(ξ) − n(A∪B) = 50 − 38 = 12

questions.question: A = {2,4,6,8,10} and B = {1,2,3,4,5}. What is n(A∩B)?
questions.explanation: A∩B = {2,4} — only 2 and 4 appear in both sets. n(A∩B) = 2.

questions.question: In a Venn diagram with two circles, how many distinct regions are there (including outside)?
questions.explanation: 4 regions: A only, A∩B (overlap), B only, and outside both circles.

questions.question: 50 people were surveyed. 35 drink tea, 20 drink coffee, 10 drink both. How many drink tea only?
questions.explanation: Tea only = 35 − 10 = 25
